#### [Traffic violation scams switch to QR codes in new phishing texts](https://www.bleepingcomputer.com/news/security/traffic-violation-scams-switch-to-qr-codes-in-new-phishing-texts/)



Scammers are sending fake "Notice of Default" traffic violation text messages impersonating state courts across the U.S., pressuring recipients to scan a QR code that leads to a phishing site demanding a $6.99 payment while stealing personal and financial information.

__llm_logo_reasoning.reasoning: Selected #0 because it is visible, located in the header, links to the homepage, and the alt text matches the brand name 'BleepingComputer'.

primary.reasoning: The 'Sign up' button is the primary CTA due to its vibrant blue color (#5177B6), which aligns with the brand's accent color, and its action-oriented text encouraging user engagement.

secondary.reasoning: The 'Login' button is selected as secondary because it has a different vibrant color (green #47851E) and serves a supportive action to the primary CTA.

personality.targetAudience: tech-savvy individuals interested in cybersecurity and technology news
